tasks.subject: [tool-result-truncation] [P1] Add inline truncation to tool-arguments-formatter fallback paths
tasks.description:
Add inline character-based truncation to the two raw JSON fallback `
` blocks in `tool-arguments-formatter.tsx`. These paths render when JSON parsing fails or when the parsed result is not a plain object. Since `TruncatedOutput` lives in the features layer and `tool-arguments-formatter.tsx` is in the shared layer, importing it would violate FSD layer rules. Instead, apply a simple inline slice with ellipsis.

## File: `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/tool-arguments-formatter.tsx`

### Change 1: JSON parse failure fallback (line 82)

Replace:
```tsx
    return 
{input}
;
```

With:
```tsx
    const displayInput = input.length > 5120 ? input.slice(0, 5120) + '\u2026' : input;
    return 
{displayInput}
;
```

### Change 2: Non-object parsed result fallback (line 86)

Replace:
```tsx
    return 
{input}
;
```

With:
```tsx
    const displayInput = input.length > 5120 ? input.slice(0, 5120) + '\u2026' : input;
    return 
{displayInput}
;
```

Note: Both lines currently have the same code `{input}`. After the change, both will use the truncated `displayInput`. The `5120` constant is inlined here rather than imported because: (1) it's a simple value, (2) importing from the features layer would violate FSD rules, and (3) the shared layer `tool-arguments-formatter.tsx` should not depend on feature-layer constants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The JSON parse failure path (catch block, line 82) truncates input at 5120 characters with a trailing ellipsis (Unicode \u2026)
- [ ] The non-object parsed result path (line 86) truncates input at 5120 characters with a trailing ellipsis
- [ ] Input under 5120 characters renders fully without ellipsis
- [ ] No new imports are added to the file
- [ ] No FSD layer violations are introduced
tasks.activeForm: Adding inline truncation to tool-arguments-formatter fallback paths
